events.label: reasoning
events.text: {"decision": "fail", "risk": "high", "work_stage": "in-service", "component_conditions": ["installed-defective", "non-compliant"], "percent_complete": 100, "value": 0, "sound_source": "", "confidence": 0.95, "event_time": 0.0, "rate": 0.0, "order": [], "workable": [], "findings": ["Return-air grille in garage-facing wall creates direct pathway for vehicle exhaust, gasoline vapors, and other garage contaminants to enter living space.", "Single-wall vent connector has only ~1 inch clearance to combustible wood framing; IRC M1801 requires 6 inches for single-wall connector.", "Condensate tube running upward before drain creates trap that can block flow, causing water backup into furnace or air handler."], "actions": ["Immediately shut down furnace and lock out power.", "Seal return-air opening in garage wall and relocate return to interior conditioned space.", "Replace single-wall vent connector with Type B gas vent or provide required 6-inch clearance to combustibles.", "Re-route condensate drain with continuous downward slope to floor drain."], "rationale": "The return-air intake from the garage is a life-safety hazard due to potential carbon monoxide and fume entrainment. The vent connector clearance violation is a fire hazard. The condensate trap can cause water damage and mold. These defects make the system unsafe to operate.", "references": ["IRC M1801.1", "IRC M1801.3", "NFPA 54 (National Fuel Gas Code) venting clearances", "IMC 918 (condensate disposal)"]}
